title: Enable lint for Darwin and fix identified issues
body:
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] Just fixing lint issues

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Error in [OSX Cross](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5520036685676544?logs=lint#L13):
```
fatal: Not a valid object name main
```
Source of error: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/07613dd84aed9644bcd428c68b2ec6bbc5882f84/Makefile#L30

Solution: beats me. Is the Macintosh git checkout somehow incomplete? Missing `main`? Is it different from regular git checkout? Needs a Mac expert.
@cevich Any idea here? We have full Git + a full repo checkout on OS X right?

This is curious, I've been seeing that message for a while now but it's never been fatal until recently.  It's possible git or bash was updated during a recent instantiation.

Another idea: It's possible there is no "main" branch cloned.  This task is using the Cirrus-CI built-in git-clone operation.  We don't use that in most other tasks, we have a custom `clone_script`.  Might simply need to `git remote update origin` or somesuch.

Suggestion: [SSH into one of the machines](https://github.com/containers/automation/blob/main/mac_pw_pool/InstanceSSH.sh) and look in `${INST_NAME}-worker/ci/task-XYZ/` to see whatsup.  Currently running [instance names are listed on the pool status page](https://cirrus-ci.com/pool/1cf8c7f7d7db0b56aecd89759721d2e710778c523a8c91c7c3aaee5b15b48d05).

>never been fatal until recently

This PR is what makes it fatal, because this PR makes lint pass, so things get farther, and `EPOCH_TEST_COMMIT` is now required.

```bash
_gc='git config --file /root/.gitconfig'
$_gc user.email "TMcTestFace@example.com"
error: could not lock config file /root/.gitconfig: No such file or directory

Exit status: 255
```

Ahh there's the problem.  Change that to `--file $HOME/.gitconfig` and it should work.  I think.

Edit: Err, on the Linux side I think maybe $HOME isn't defined at this level :thinking: 

Edit 2: It's worse because `contrib/cirrus/mac_setup.sh` changes the value of `$HOME` :cry: 

Edit 3: I think I see an easy way to fix this, let me give it a whirl in a separate PR...https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21777
Confirmed, https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21777 will fix the cloning problem.  Can you try rebasing this PR on it?
OSX Cross test is failing with apparent Lint issues.
Alright, going to have to conditionalize pre-commit on non-OSX

Just a reminder: /hold is no longer necessary.  PRs cannot merge w/o a green "Total Success".

(Though I get there are other reasons to hold, so no worries).

Let's test that here, since this is low impact
/hold cancel
Rebasing to pick up the HyperV fix
